slides.title: 14-Export of IGP Algorithm Information in IPFIX

polls.text: Do you object adopting the draft?

chat.author: Yingzhen Qu
chat.text: @meetecho, camera at the presenter please

chat.author: Shraddha Hegde
chat.text: voice too low on the bridge

chat.author: Lorenzo Miniero
chat.text: I think the mic is turned off

chat.author: Lorenzo Miniero
chat.text: Can someone in the room tell the speaker?

chat.author: Shraddha Hegde
chat.text: Its better now

chat.author: Yingzhen Qu
chat.text: mic is on, but weak @meetecho

chat.author: John Scudder
chat.text: Turns out mic works better when you switch it on. :-o

chat.author: Les Ginsberg
chat.text: Cannot hear the speaker

chat.author: Yingzhen Qu
chat.text: please help with the note taking: https://notes.ietf.org/notes-ietf-126-lsr?both

chat.author: Shraddha Hegde
chat.text: cannot hear the question

chat.author: Vishnu Beeram
chat.text: mic just got turned on..

chat.author: Boris Khasanov
chat.text: cool

chat.author: John Scudder
chat.text: See previous comment.

chat.author: John Scudder
chat.text: Sometimes there are oopsies related to allocation of code points from informational RFCs so be careful.

chat.author: John Scudder
chat.text: Depends on registry policy.

chat.author: John Scudder
chat.text: Surely the way to determine if the model is right or not is to review the document and critique it on the merits. This kind of meta critique doesn’t seem helpful.

chat.author: John Scudder
chat.text: Which would be a reason to adopt it.

chat.author: John Scudder
chat.text: It would be helpful to know WHY those objecting, take that position.

chat.author: John Scudder
chat.text: I don’t think it would be a problem holding up the adoption IF the code points allocation weren’t being gated on adoption. But as it is, an objection to adoption is effectively an objection to running code.

chat.author: Vishnu Beeram
chat.text: draft-many-teas-power-steering is on the TEAS WG agenda this wednesday; Please be there to discuss and dissect the power group construct (or send your comments to the TEAS list)

chat.author: John Scudder
chat.text: Is there a computer scientist in the house? ;-)

chat.author: Tony Li
chat.text: To Chris' point: there are customers that care about security far more than functionality.

chat.author: Robert Raszuk
chat.text: How is this "offloading small portion of the traffic" if this is per destination and in vast majority of the domains traffic is encapsulated so this would be per egress node granularity
